title: IDE-IPA-Railway: ALIVE Framework V2.0 MCP Server for Innovation-Driven Enterprise Assessment
description: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server implementing the ALIVE Framework V2.0 (Agentic Longitudinal IDE-IPA Value Evaluation) for evaluating Innovation-Driven Enterprises (IDEs) under Thailand's PMUC funding scheme. Architecture: 100-point rubric scoring across four parts - Part A (Standard IDE Assessment, 28 points), Part B (Research-Specific Assessment, 22 points), Part C (Impact Pathway Logic / Ex-Ante Analysis, 25 points), Part D (SROI Assessment, 25 points). Features the PACT (Plan-Act-Check-Track) longitudinal coaching loop with auto-calibrating halting, dual-database architecture combining PostgreSQL for assessment history with ChromaDB for semantic memory using paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 (384-dim, Thai-English bilingual), L6 Knowledge Broker tools for cross-agent semantic learning and peer matching, self-evaluation loop with task-specific keyword matching, and production deployment on Railway with FastMCP transport. Version 2.1.2 introduces idempotent l6_kb_extract_lessons with deterministic MD5-hash atom_ids, eliminating duplicate atoms in shared_kb on retry. This Zenodo archive captures the exact codebase used to generate empirical results reported in the associated ALIVE Framework academic paper.

creators.name: Numsomran, Arjin
